entry.title: Lessons Learned from Hypercare to AMS Transition in S/4HANA Projects
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In several SAP S/4HANA projects, I have observed that the transition from hypercare to AMS support is often more challenging than anticipated, even when KT activities, documentation, and sign-offs are completed successfully.

Systems may appear stable during the early go-live period, but operational issues can surface later during peak business cycles such as month-end processing, interface spikes, or batch job overlaps.

Some areas that I personally found important during AMS transition include:

  • Understanding real production behaviour during peak load
  • Identifying critical monitoring periods
  • Capturing lessons learned from hypercare
  • Enhancing coordination between project and AMS teams
  • Building AMS team troubleshooting confidence before full takeover

In certain situations, temporary workarounds or manual monitoring activities performed during hypercare were not fully documented during KT, which later created operational ambiguity for support teams.

I would like to hear from the community:

  • What are the key challenges you have seen during Hypercare to AMS transition in S/4HANA projects?
  • Are there any best practices your teams follow to improve operational readiness and long-term support stability?

I look forward to hearing different experiences and approaches from the community.

Best regards,
Raghu Garapati

entry.title: Subject: Error when updating existing G/L Accounts via Migration Cockpit (FH 59 / DMC_RT_MSG 222)
entry.content:
専門家の皆様へ

SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpitで総勘定元帳を処理する際に発生する問題について、ご助言をいただきたく存じます。

■ エラーメッセージ

  • FH 59: 勘定科目 XXXX は既に勘定科目表 ZJCO に存在します
  • DMC_RT_MSG 188
  • DMC_RT_MSG 222: レコードをターゲットシステムに転送できません

■ システム／オブジェクト

  • 移行対象: FI - 総勘定元帳
  • 勘定科目一覧：ZJCO
  • 会社コード: J901

■現状

  • 総勘定科目は全て既に存在します（FS00で確認済み）。
  • 私の目的は既存の記録を更新することであり、新しい記録を作成することではありません。
  • 修正ファイルを使用して「置換」でアップロードしています。

■私が確認した／行ったこと

  • 会社コードと勘定科目表は適切に定義されています
  • アカウントグループの設定（OBD4）が完了しました
  • フィールドステータスバリアントはOBY6で確認されています
  • 割り当てられたバリアント内で、OBC4 のフィールドステータスグループが正しく維持されています。
  • フィールドステータスバリアントの不一致は既に解決済みです
  • 移行ファイルの準備：
    • 必須項目のみ入力されています
    • 必須項目でないフィールドは空白のままです
    • 非表示フィールドには値が入力されていません（フィールドの状態に基づきます）
    • 日付と数値の形式を確認済み

■問題点
上記にもかかわらず、システムはレコードを更新するのではなく作成しようとしているかのように動作し、結果として以下の問題が発生します。

  • FH 59（既に存在します）
  • 続いて DMC_RT_MSG 222 (転送できません)

■私の質問

  1. Migration Cockpitを使用して既存の総勘定科目を更新する正しい方法は何ですか？
  2. 更新時（新規作成時ではなく）の修正ファイルの処理方法は？
  3. FH 59とDMC_RT_MSG 222が同時に発生する典型的な原因は何ですか？
  4. アップデート機能を有効にするために、何か特別な設定や手順が必要ですか？

■補足事項
私の理解では：

  • データの一貫性が確保されています
  • フィールドの状態と構成が一致しています
  • したがって、これは処理モード（作成か更新か）に関係しているのではないかと推測されます。

何かアドバイスやベストプラクティスがあれば教えていただけると幸いです。

どうもありがとうございます。

entry.title: Re: Where to create new FS item : Dev with transport to Q...
entry.content: Hi Martin, You are right that in past times we usually did not transport FS items in FSV. Especially, after incorporating Group Reporting, SAP has made it more possible to transport Financial Statement (FS) items in SAP, particularly for S/4HANA Group Reporting. This involves using the Customizing Transport Request mechanism to move master data and configuration from development to quality/production. Nowadays, Group Reporting is widely being included in the mainstream of SAP. Any changes carried out in S/4HANA, to Define FS Items Fiori app (F3297) should automatically prompt for a transport request if the system is configured for Automatic Recording of Changes. Kindly ensure that attributes such as FS Item Type (BS vs P&L), elimination rules, and breakdown categories are correctly captured in the transport. Regards, Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to split PPV when posting a Goods Rece...
entry.content:
Hi Goody-1

Yes, there are several ways to split Purchase Price Variance (PPV) when posting a goods receipt (GR) in SAP, depending on whether you want to split the accounting entries, the material valuation, or the physically received quantity.

Finance Material Valuation:
In SAP S/4HANA, you can configure the system to automatically split price differences into different General Ledger (GL) accounts based on variance categories. This provides more granular visibility in your Profit and Loss (P&L) statement.

1. Usually PRD account is used to reverse the initial posting in SAP. So, instead of all variances hitting a single PRD account, if separate GL accounts are created for each variance category then PRD values can be split.

2. Define a Price Difference Split Profile in the SPRO path:
IMG Financial Accounting > General Ledger Accounting > Periodic Processing > Integration > Materials Management
3. Assign the splitting profile to your company code

Inventory Split can also be achieved using the Split Valuation in SAP-MM

Regards,

Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content: Hi Rohit - When the "Inbound service not found" error for SAP UDM Collections Management indicates that the OData service (UDMO_COLLECTION_WORKLIST) is not activated in transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE or that a service group binding is missing, causing a 404/MED033 error. Please Consult your technical team. Regards, PS

entry.title: Errors during Read Jobs execution in Identity Provisioning - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition activation
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently in the process of activating a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant. As part of the initial setup, we accessed the administrator console, created an additional user beyond the default admin user, and proceeded to execute the Read Jobs for the different source systems configured in the Identity Provisioning Service (IPS).

During the execution of the Read Jobs for the systems S4-my438910-source and IAS formy438924 - source, we encountered the following errors:

Error type 1 — User provisioning failures:

 
 
user:56309a19-64ec-45b8-92d3-1ef6fe509112,system=S4HANAmy438910 - target,time=2026-05-05T18:47:19.646+00,error=Person SALES.ADOPTION@STRUCTUM-CO.COM does not exist.,content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities
 
 
user:63a9dc7c-f700-4521-ba3b-d6e44de2ea2e,system=S4HANAmy438910 - target,time=2026-05-05T18:47:19.816+00,error=Person OSCAR.QUIJANO@STRUCTUM-CO.COM does not exist.,content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

Error type 2 — Group provisioning failures (7 similar errors):

 
 
group:my39467937_SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_PROJECT_LEAD,system=S4HANAmy438910 - target,time=2026-05-05T18:47:20.174+00,error=HTTP operation failed invoking https://my438910-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/APS_IAM_SIAG_BROLE_SRV/Aps_Iam_Siag_Br_Dll('my39467937_SAP_CBC_CONSUMPTION_PROJECT_LEAD')?$select=ID,to_BusinessUserAssignment/PersonID,Description,%20to_BusinessUserAssignment/UserName&$expand=to_BusinessUserAssignment with statusCode: 400, Response: {"error":{"code":"005056A509B11EE1B9A8DBD9EA7BB778","message":{"lang":"en","value":"Malformed URI literal syntax"},"innererror":{"transactionid":"9FCE19A6C2AB341BE25A425D99E4EB99","timestamp":"20260505184720.1502620","Error_Resolution":{"SAP_Transaction":"For backend administrators: use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details","SAP_Note":"See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)"}}}},content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

Environment details:

  • Product: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Process: Initial tenant activation
  • Component: Identity Provisioning Service (IPS)
  • Source systems involved: S4-my438910-source, IAS formy438924 - source
  • Target system: S4HANAmy438910 - target
  • No custom transformations or filters have been applied — all systems are using predefined SAP configurations

We have not made any custom changes to the transformations or properties of these systems beyond what SAP provisions by default. We are looking for guidance from experienced consultants or SAP experts who have gone through this activation process and can help us understand:

  1. Why are users being reported as non-existent when they were created in the system?
  2. What is the recommended corrective action without deviating from the standard SAP-initiated configuration?

Any help or pointers to relevant SAP Notes or documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Project Systems How to Change a WBS element after it...
entry.content:
Hi Margie,
Chris has explained the process well. An alternate solution would be to:
Sometimes, we have seen things getting stuck in SAP because the response from the user was unacceptable. Since you are unable to do anything with WBS ID correction, you may try these steps:

1. Ensure that CJI3 is pulling information as Project Actual Cost Line Items, and you are unable to reverse the posting as explained by Chris.
2. KB11n - Manual Reposting of Costs to transfer primary costs from one cost object to another without reversing the original financial document.

  • Use Scr Variant - 'WBS element/order' and input type 'List Entry'
  • Put WBS element (old) with cost element and Put WBS element (new) with quantity if applicable

3. Now you can run CJ88 - Settlement of WBS

4. Note: Previous Year Issues: If the settlement occurred in a previous fiscal year, reversal is more complex and may affect depreciation.

Regards, Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Re: When One Release Has to Reach Many SAP Cloud Integration Tenants
entry.content:
Hi @dougliu 

I’ve worked with both approaches depending on the client maturity and landscape setup.

For some clients, we use SAP Cloud Transport Management (TMS) to move artifacts across DEV/QA/PRD tenants in a more controlled and governed way. But I’ve also worked with newer SAP IS clients where transports are still handled manually by downloading the iFlow/package ZIPs and importing them into the target tenant.

Honestly, I still see a lot of customers operating in a semi-manual way, especially smaller teams or environments that were initially set up quickly and later scaled.

I personally haven’t worked with Content Agent Service (CAS) yet, so I can’t speak much on real-world adoption there from my side yet.

entry.title: Серія вебкастів SAP у травні
entry.content:
Травень — насичений місяць для SAP-спільноти! Найбільша подія року та експертні вебкасти з безпеки, оновлень та інтеграції — ми зібрали всі ключові можливості для навчання в одному місці.

Обирайте найцікавіші для вас сесії та реєструйтесь за лінками нижче.

Час вказано за Києвом | Мова вебінарів — англійська.

AI, інновації та майбутнє SAP

  • 12 - 13 травня
    SAP Sapphire Virtual
    12 травня: початок о 15:30, остання сесія завершується о 23:45
    13 травня: початок о 16:00, остання сесія завершується о 23:30

    Найбільша подія SAP року — тепер у віртуальному форматі, наживо. Приєднуйтесь, щоб побачити сміливе бачення SAP щодо того, як бізнес працюватиме в майбутньому.
    Головна тема цього року: The Beginning of Better — переосмислення того, як працює бізнес в епоху AI. У центрі уваги — оновлений Joule: новий центральний робочий простір, який проактивно покращує бізнес-операції та об'єднує людей і AI для досягнення реальних результатів.
    З вітальним кейнотом виступлять CEO Christian Klein та топ-керівники SAP: Muhammad Alam - член виконавчого комітету, Sebastian Steinhaeuser – COO, Philipp Herzig - CTO. Вони розкажуть, як SAP забезпечує потужну основу з застосунків та даних, яка дозволяє AI досягати реальних бізнес-результатів.
    🎾 Спеціальний гість — Roger Federer 13 травня о 22:30 - 23:30 поділиться інсайтами про стійкість та стратегічне мислення для лідерів бізнесу у будь-якій галузі.

    Що на вас чекає: живі демонстрації оновленого Joule та AI-агентів, live "Ask the Expert" Q&A сесії з експертами SAP, тематичні треки: AI, Cloud ERP, Joule, Data & Analytics, Supply Chain, Build & Integrate.
    🎓 Бонус для учасників: 25% знижка на SAP Certification та безкоштовний доступ до SAP Learning Hub, event edition.
    Детальніше про спікерів та повний каталог сесій — за посиланням. Використовуйте фільтри та пошук, щоб знайти найцікавіші для вас сесії, додавайте їх до персонального розкладу та відстежуйте в особистому кабінеті. Всі прямі трансляції будуть доступні у записі після події.
    Додавайте до календаря — SAP Sapphire 2026 це не просто подія, це точка відліку нової ери бізнесу з AI. 🚀

Безпека SAP

  • 21 травня, 11:30 – 12:30
    SAP Security Webcast in May: Quantum Safe Cryptography – Surviving the Quantum Apocalypse - EMEA
    Щомісяця SAP обирає найактуальніші теми з безпеки для клієнтів. У травні — квантова криптографія та її вплив на ваше SAP-середовище.
    Квантові комп'ютери вже найближчим часом можуть зламати основу сучасної криптографії. Дізнайтесь, що це означає для SAP бізнес-застосунків, які варіанти захисту існують, та як спланувати post-quantum cryptography міграцію. В результаті — готовий план дій для захисту SAP-ландшафту в епоху квантових технологій.
    Записи попередніх вебкастів доступні за посиланням.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • 21 травня, 16:15 – 17:00
    In-Depth with SAP Cloud ERP May Edition – What's New in SAP Central Business Configuration
    Огляд останніх оновлень та нових можливостей SAP Central Business Configuration. У програмі: огляд нових функцій, живі демонстрації та сесія Q&A. Sales, project managers, presales, consultants та business users можуть ознайомитися з найновішими можливостями SAP CBC.

Інтеграція

  • 26 травня, 17:00 – 18:00
    SAP Integration Suite - Monthly Update May
    SAP Integration Suite — це iPaaS платформа для проектування, розробки, виконання та управління інтеграційними потоками, API та бізнес-подіями, що з'єднують хмарні та локальні процеси, застосунки і дані.
    У програмі: Product Updates та інтерактивна сесія з експертом — Integration Black Belt або #AskAnythingIntegration, де панель експертів відповість на ваші запитання.

Травень — це місяць, коли SAP-спільнота збирається разом: навчається, ділиться досвідом та готується до майбутнього. Не пропустіть жодної можливості — реєструйтесь, додавайте до календаря та приєднуйтесь! 💡

Extracting Details of Created Promotions

entry.title: Important Update: What's Changing in US Federal Regulatory Tax Reporting and How SAP has you covered
entry.content:
OVERVIEW
What SAP Users Need to Know About the Tax Year 2026/Reporting Year 2027 Transition for IRS Reporting.

As we move into 2026, the IRS is implementing one of the most significant updates to its information returns filing infrastructure in decades. The legacy Filing Information Returns Electronically (FIRE) system, which many of you currently use for submitting 1099 forms and other informational reports, will retire on December 31, 2026.

Starting with Tax Year 2026 filings (due in early 2027), form submissions must be made through the Information Returns Intake System (IRIS). This change directly impacts how SAP customers generate, validate, and file forms such as 1099-MISC, 1099-INT, 1099-NEC, 1099-K, 1099-G, 1099-C, 1042-S, 1099-R, and 1098-T using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition/Private Edition/On-premise. SAP ERP (ECC) is supported by ABAP Program (RFIDYYWT).

We are here to help you navigate this transition smoothly. Below, we outline the key changes, benefits, and actionable steps to ensure continued compliance with minimal disruption.

JOIN OUR UPCOMING WEBINARS

SAP has planned two webinars for customers and partners which will provide an overview and awareness about the IRIS Transition. Please plan to attend one of these:

• June 2, 2026, 11 AM PST on SAP ON24 - Registration link here
• June 9, 2026, 11 AM PST at ASUG - Registration link here

WHY IRS IS MOVING FROM FIRE TO IRIS

The FIRE system, built on decades-old technology, is being replaced by IRIS — a modern, web-based platform designed to improve accuracy, security, and efficiency.

Key Differences:

AspectFIRE(Legacy)IRIS(New)
File FormatsFlat text files (Publication 1220 format)Structured XML for automated e-filing (A2A) or CSV for portal uploads
ValidationPost-submission rejectionsReal-time error checking and instant corrections
TCC RequirementExisting FIRE TCCNew IRIS-specific TCC required (starting with 'D')
Filing OptionsLimited automationIRIS Taxpayer Portal (manual/CSV uploads: up to 250 records per file, unlimited files) or XML A2A for high-volume automated filing

IMPORTANT: You'll need a separate IRIS-specific Transmitter Control Code (TCC) starting with 'D'. Existing FIRE TCCs won't work — apply early, as approval can take up to 45 days.

FIRE and IRIS will run in parallel during 2026 (for Tax Year 2025 filings due this year), giving you time to test and prepare. However, after December 31, 2026, FIRE will no longer accept submissions and reports must be filed via IRIS.

HOW THIS AFFECTS SAP CUSTOMERS

SAP continues to enhance SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) to align with evolving U.S. statutory requirements, including 1099 and 1042-S reporting. SAP-ERP Reports will also be enhanced to comply with IRIS regulations.

Key Change: No More Copy-A

A key change with the IRIS transition: Copy-A (the IRS-facing red-ink or pre-printable version for paper submission) is no longer necessary and will not be supported.

Two Primary Electronic Filing Methods

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance will provide:

1. CSV file generation — Optimized for direct upload to the IRIS Taxpayer Portal (ideal for manual filing scenarios)
Note: This option is supported only for Tax Year 2026/Filing Year 2027 to facilitate a smooth transition. It will be deprecated in Tax Year 2027 onwards.

2. Direct electronic filing via XML — For secure, automated submission through IRIS Application-to-Application (A2A) communication

Recipient copies (Copy-B PDFs) will continue to be fully supported for distribution to payees/recipients, ensuring you meet furnishing requirements without interruption.

Release Timeline

To support a smooth rollout, SAP plans to release updated DRC functionality for all reports by early October 2026. This will give you sufficient time to install, test, and be ready by early 2027.

This approach gives customers ample time to run thorough testing with the IRIS Assurance Testing System (ATS) during the parallel operation period in 2026 — well before the mandatory shift for Tax Year 2026 filings in 2027.

OUTPUT SUPPORT SUMMARY BY SAP SOLUTION

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition/Private Edition/On-Premise

Legal outputs to be filed with IRIS:

✓ Download of CSV file (Tax Year 2026/Filing Year 2027 only, This output will be deprecated beyond tax year 2026.
✓ E-filing via XML (requires DRC Cloud Edition license - contact your account executives for pricing)
✗ Copy-A (not supported - no longer necessary)

Business partner correspondence:
✓ Copy-B (fully supported)

SAP ERP (ECC)

Legal outputs to be filed with IRIS:
✓ Download of CSV file
✗ E-filing (not supported due to platform limitations)
✗ Copy-A (not supported - no longer necessary)

Business partner correspondence:
✓ Copy-B (fully supported)

BENEFITS YOU'LL SEE WITH IRIS VIA SAP DRC

• Fewer rejections and faster processing thanks to real-time validation
• Larger file capacities and automated bulk filing for high-volume submitters
• Better data accuracy with structured XML/CSV formats, reducing manual rework
• Long-term alignment with IRS modernization — positioning your organization for future form additions and compliance changes

IMMEDIATE NEXT STEPS FOR YOUR TEAM

1. Apply for an IRIS TCC Now

Visit the IRS IRIS portal and submit your application (irs.gov → search "IRIS Application for TCC").

Required Reading:
• IRS Publications 5717, 5718, and 5719
• IRIS 101 and tutorial PDF (provides step-by-step instructions)

Do this ASAP to avoid delays in early 2027!

2. Review Your SAP DRC License and Configuration

Review your current 1099/1042-S/1099-R/1098-T report setups in DRC. Note that new configurations will be required for IRIS compliance.

3. Test Early

Use the time in 2026 to:
• Generate sample IRIS outputs (XML)
• Submit test filings through the IRIS Assurance Testing System (ATS)

Note: IRS will allow you to submit in production only after certification in their ATS system. IRS has not provided an ATS environment for CSV uploads.

4. Stay Updated

Monitor SAP Notes, SAP Community posts on DRC and U.S. withholding.

IRS RESOURCES

• IRIS Portal: irs.gov (search 'IRIS')
• TCC Application: 'IRIS Application for TCC'
- You will need Issuer & Transmitter roles
- TCC Application Tutorial
- After TCC approval, apply for API Client ID for XML (A2A) transmissions
- Complete A2A consent with IRS for e-filing

Key Publications:
• 5717: Taxpayer Portal User Guide
• 5718: Electronic Filing Application to Application (A2A) Specifications
• 5719: Test Package for Information Returns
• Follow "Submit ATS transmissions" to move from Test to Prod
• Instruction 1099-R and 5498

JOIN OUR COMMUNITY WORKSPACES

Stay connected and get the latest updates:

1. Open the Workspace link: USA  — SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
2. USA – SAP Human Capital Management Localization
3. Click the "Request to Join" button
4. Use your work email to submit the request
5. Include your company name, your full name, and your role in the comment section

Subscribe to Regulatory Change Manager for US to follow the releases.

FINAL THOUGHTS

The shift to IRIS is an opportunity to modernize your reporting processes — and with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance's ongoing enhancements and phased rollout, you will be well-equipped to stay compliant effortlessly.

If you have questions about how this affects your specific SAP setup or need help with preparing, please contact our support team.

Thank you for your continued partnership. We're here to support you through this important transition.

Product Management, SAP Globalization

entry.title: Unable to send reports to external user emails
entry.content:
I’m unable to send reports to external email addresses and receive the following error. Internal and cross‑tenant emails send successfully.

Error Message:
  • address error. [CrystalEnterprise.Smtp]: [Error sending address(es) to SMTP server. Return code: [SMTP 451 - Requested action aborted: local error in processing.]. Reason: [userxx@gmail.com].]

In AJS, I confirmed that the connection security is using STARTTLS. I also verified that sending external emails directly from Exchange works without any issues, so Exchange isn’t blocking outbound mail.

Nothing appears in the message trace — the messages aren’t even reaching Exchange. I’m wondering if Microsoft is rejecting the emails before they get that far. I’ve added the server’s public IP to the DNS SPF record and confirmed that the Exchange connector is using the correct server IP.

entry.title: Re: EWM - Modifying custom fields in HU header using fm /...
entry.content:
Hi,

The function module changes the HU buffer.

The BAdI is not the correct one and you shouldn't be using it in order to make changes in the core data. It is very likely that your changes in the HU buffer are just not saved by the standard call stack, since the BAdI is called to check goods movements for a delivery.

The correct BAdIs for your updates would be /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_HU_SAVE (should be called during update task processing of the WT confirmation, which is a GM posting in this case) or, less preferably, even /SCWM/EX_DLV_DET_AT_SAVE. You should quickly check beforehand if /SCWM/EX_CORE_CO_HU_SAVE is actually called during your postings. Therefore, do a where-used on the BAdI and set an external breakpoint (set the debugger to update task debugging, too) before the BAdI call. This will enable you to see if the BAdI is called during your postings. If the mentioned BAdI is good for your use case, don't use the FM inside. Instead, you have to set the Z fields in CS_DEST inside the BAdI and let standard handle the rest.

Cheers,

Jonas

entry.title: Re: Product wt
entry.content:
Hi, you can:

- set the pick HU control for the storage type of the pick point (/SCWM/T331-PIKTA) to 3 or 2 (and then set it the process type to 3 accordingly, /SCWM/T333-PIKTA)

- deactivate proposal of pick-HUs (which you don't have here)

- check during WT confirmation that HUENT (HU withdrawal) is not set automatically by standard

Confirm the WT directly to the staging area. Since you don't have a pick HU (not the full-HU and not a newly created one), system won't try to create follow-up WT for the pick-HU.

Cheers,

Jonas

entry.title: SAP APIM Logging in Splunk
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP API Management often requires centralized transaction logging to support monitoring, troubleshooting, and operational analysis across API flows. While working with SAP APIM, I found it difficult to consistently monitor request and response errors and track client IP addresses, especially when issues had to be investigated across multiple policies and runtime stages. On one implementation, this became a recurring support problem because we could see that requests were failing, but we could not quickly tell whether the failure happened during API key validation, target processing, or response handling. That experience led me to implement a centralized logging pattern with Splunk. In this scenario, the goal was to capture key request and response details, including validation failures, response status, and performance metrics, without introducing significant runtime overhead. This post describes a reusable integration pattern using KVM-based configuration, asynchronous service callout, and PreFlow/PostFlow logging so that structured API logging can be implemented in SAP APIM for improved observability.

Integration Pattern for SAP APIM and Splunk

This approach was chosen to keep the logging design secure, reusable, and lightweight. Splunk endpoint details and authentication values are externalized in KVM so they can be maintained without changing policy code. Logging is separated across PreFlow and PostFlow to capture both early validation failures and end-to-end response details, while asynchronous service callouts help minimize runtime impact on the API transaction.

How to integrate Splunk with SAP APIM?
Prerequisites
Splunk URL: https://{{HostName}}:{{Port}}/services/collector/event
Authorization header: Splunk {{Token}}
Data format: JSON
{
    "index": "test_sap",
    "host": "cpidev",
    "sourcetype": "sap:api:events",
    "source": "API00XX",
    "event": {
        "message": "Event Data/Structure Here"
    }
}
Solution Overview

The implementation uses SAP APIM policies and JavaScript to build a structured log payload and forward it to Splunk. PreFlow handles authentication-related failures before the request reaches the backend, while PostFlow captures response status, payload details, and timing metrics after target processing. This separation is useful because it ensures that both failed and completed transactions can be logged based on configured conditions. It also gives the team flexibility to log only exceptions or all traffic, depending on operational needs and expected log volume.

Policy Steps: PreFlow
KVM: SplunkDetails (Connection Settings)

This policy reads the Splunk connection details from a Key Value Map, including the URL, resource path, host, index, source type, and proxy-specific success logging flag. KVM is used here to externalize configuration so that endpoint changes can be handled without modifying policy definitions or JavaScript logic. If any required KVM entry is missing at runtime, the downstream service callout may fail or produce incomplete log events, so these values should be validated carefully in each environment.

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="SplunkDetails" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true"
    xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <!-- PUT stores the key value pair mentioned inside the element -->
    <Get assignTo="private.splunkURL">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>splunkURL</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.splunkResourcePath">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>splunkResourcePath</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.splunkHost">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>splunkHost</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.splunkIndex">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>splunkIndex</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.splunkSourceType">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>splunkSourceType</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <Get assignTo="private.sendSucessToSplunk">
        <Key>
            <Parameter ref="apiproxy.name" />
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <!-- the scope of the key value map. Valid values are environment, organization, apiproxy and policy -->
</KeyValueMapOperations>
KVM: Passwords (Token)

This policy retrieves the Splunk token from a secure Key Value Map entry. Storing the token outside the policy definition helps reduce hardcoding of credentials and makes token rotation easier to manage. If the token is invalid or missing, the call to Splunk will not be authorized, so this is one of the first places to check during troubleshooting.

<KeyValueMapOperations mapIdentifier="Passwords" async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true"
    xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <!-- PUT stores the key value pair mentioned inside the element -->
    <Get assignTo="private.splunkToken">
        <Key>
            <Parameter>SPLUNKLOGGING_TOKEN</Parameter>
        </Key>
    </Get>
    <!-- the scope of the key value map. Valid values are environment, organization, apiproxy and policy -->
</KeyValueMapOperations>

JavaScript

This JavaScript policy constructs the structured log payload for authentication failures detected in PreFlow. It captures request metadata such as API key, product name, proxy name, environment, client IP address, timestamps, request details, and the validation fault name. This step is important because authentication failures occur before the backend is invoked, so if they are not logged in PreFlow, they may be missed entirely in transaction monitoring.

var logdata = {
    apiKey: context.getVariable("request.header.APIKey"),
    productName: context.getVariable("apiproduct.name"),
    proxyName: context.getVariable("apiproxy.name"),
    environmentName: context.getVariable("environment.name"),
    IpAddress: context.getVariable("client.ip"),
    currentSystemTime: context.getVariable("system.time"),
    currentSystemTimeStamp: context.getVariable("system.timestamp"),
    requestReceivedStartTime: context.getVariable("client.received.start.time"),
    requestReceivedStartTimeStamp: context.getVariable("client.received.start.timestamp"),
    requestReceivedEndTime: context.getVariable("client.received.end.time"),
    requestReceivedEndtTimeStamp: context.getVariable("client.received.end.timestamp"),
    responseCode: "401",
    messageId: context.getVariable("messageid"),
    requestURI: context.getVariable("request.uri"),
    requestVerb: context.getVariable("request.verb"),
    requestContent: context.getVariable("request.content"),
    responseMessage: context.getVariable("oauthV2.VAK-ValidateAPIKey.fault.name")
}
context.setVariable("sapapim.logmessage", JSON.stringify(logdata));

Note : Regarding the variables use the reference SAP Help Link variable-references

Service Callout

This service callout sends the PreFlow log payload to the Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoint. It is configured asynchronously so that the logging call does not unnecessarily delay the client-facing transaction. The condition should be set so that it runs only when API key or OAuth validation fails; otherwise, Splunk will receive unnecessary events and the logs will become noisy.

Condition: Use oauthV2.VAK-ValidateAPIKey.failed is true for source-side API key authentication. If OAuth-based authentication is used, replace this with oauthV2.failed is true. This condition ensures that only requests with authentication failures are sent to Splunk, which helps keep the logs focused on exception scenarios.

<ServiceCallout async="true" continueOnError="true" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <Request>
        <Set>
            <Headers>
                <Header name="Content-Type">application/json</Header>
                <Header name="Authorization">Splunk {private.splunkToken}</Header>
            </Headers>
            <Payload contentType="application/json" variablePrefix="#" variableSuffix="@">{
                "time": #client.received.start.timestamp@,
                "index": "#private.splunkIndex@",
                "host": "#private.splunkHost@",
                "sourcetype": "#private.splunkSourceType@",
                "source": "#apiproxy.name@",
                "event": {
                "Message": #sapapim.logmessage@
                }
                }</Payload>
            <Verb>POST</Verb>
        </Set>
    </Request>
    <Timeout>30000</Timeout>
    <HTTPTargetConnection>
        <URL>https://{private.splunkURL}/{private.splunkResourcePath}</URL>
    </HTTPTargetConnection>
</ServiceCallout>
Raise Fault

This policy returns a controlled error response to the client when authentication fails. It ensures that the consumer receives a consistent 401 Unauthorized response rather than an inconsistent backend or policy error. This is useful not only for client handling, but also because it keeps the error path predictable while the failure is logged separately to Splunk.

Condition: Use oauthV2.VAK-ValidateAPIKey.failed is true for source-side API key authentication. If OAuth-based authentication is used instead, replace this with oauthV2.failed is true and update the error reference in the policy to oauthV2.OA-VerifyAccessToken.fault.name.

This condition determines whether the exception flow should be triggered when authentication fails. It ensures that only failed authentication requests are handled by the fault policy, which helps maintain consistent error handling and supports accurate logging for monitoring and troubleshooting.

<!-- can be used to create custom messages in case of an error condition -->
<RaiseFault async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <!-- Defines the response message returned to the requesting client -->
    <FaultResponse>
        <Set>
            <!-- Sets or overwrites HTTP headers in the respone message -->
            <Headers />
            <Payload contentType="application/json">
                {

                "Error": "{oauthV2.VAK-ValidateAPIKey.fault.name}"

                }
            </Payload>
            <StatusCode>401</StatusCode>
            <!-- sets the reason phrase of the response -->
            <ReasonPhrase>Unauthorized</ReasonPhrase>
        </Set>
    </FaultResponse>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</RaiseFault>

Policy Steps: PostFlow
JavaScript

This JavaScript policy prepares the structured log payload after target processing is completed. In addition to request and response details, it captures timing information such as target response time and overall API response time, which makes the logs useful for both troubleshooting and performance analysis. This policy also reads the success logging flag so that the proxy can be configured to send only failures or both failures and successful transactions.

var sendSuccessToSplunk = context.getVariable("private.sendSucessToSplunk");
var logdata = {
    apiKey: context.getVariable("request.header.APIKey"),
    productName: context.getVariable("apiproduct.name"),
    proxyName: context.getVariable("apiproxy.name"),
    environmentName: context.getVariable("environment.name"),
    IpAddress: context.getVariable("client.ip"),
    currentSystemTime: context.getVariable("system.time"),
    currentSystemTimeStamp: context.getVariable("system.timestamp"),
    requestReceivedStartTime: context.getVariable("client.received.start.time"),
    requestReceivedStartTimeStamp: context.getVariable("client.received.start.timestamp"),
    requestReceivedEndTime: context.getVariable("client.received.end.time"),
    requestReceivedEndtTimeStamp: context.getVariable("client.received.end.timestamp"),
    targetResponseTime: context.getVariable("target.received.end.timestamp") - context.getVariable("target.sent.start.timestamp"),
    apiResponseTime: context.getVariable("system.timestamp") - context.getVariable("client.received.end.timestamp"),
    targetResponseEndTime: context.getVariable("target.received.end.time"),
    messageStatusCode: context.getVariable("message.status.code"),
    messageId: context.getVariable("messageid"),
    requestContent: context.getVariable("request.content"),
    requestURI: context.getVariable("request.uri"),
    requestVerb: context.getVariable("request.verb"),
    requestURL: context.getVariable("request.url"),
    responseMessage: context.getVariable("response.content"),
    sendSuccessToSplunk: context.getVariable("private.sendSucessToSplunk")
}
context.setVariable("sapapim.logmessage", JSON.stringify(logdata));
Service Callout

This service callout sends the PostFlow event to Splunk after the response is generated. The condition allows logging for 4xx and 5xx responses by default, and it can also log successful responses when the proxy-specific KVM setting is enabled. This design gives good operational flexibility, because teams that want lower log volume can capture only failures, while teams that need fuller observability can enable success logging.

Condition: Use ((message.status.code =| 4 or message.status.code =| 5) and private.sendSucessToSplunk is false) or private.sendSucessToSplunk is true.

This condition determines whether the transaction should be forwarded to Splunk after response processing. If success logging is disabled, only error responses in the 4xx or 5xx range are sent to Splunk. If success logging is enabled, both successful and failed transactions are logged.

The ProxyName entry in the SplunkDetails KVM controls this behavior. If the value is true, Splunk receives both success and failure messages. If the value is false, only failure messages are sent, which helps reduce log volume while still preserving visibility into exception scenarios.

<ServiceCallout async="true" continueOnError="true" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <Request>
        <Set>
            <Headers>
                <Header name="Content-Type">application/json</Header>
                <Header name="Authorization">Splunk {private.splunkToken}</Header>
            </Headers>
            <Payload contentType="application/json" variablePrefix="#" variableSuffix="@">{
                "time": #client.received.start.timestamp@,
                "index": "#private.splunkIndex@",
                "host": "#private.splunkHost@",
                "sourcetype": "#private.splunkSourceType@",
                "source": "#apiproxy.name@",
                "event": {
                "Message": #sapapim.logmessage@
                }
                }</Payload>
            <Verb>POST</Verb>
        </Set>
    </Request>
    <Timeout>30000</Timeout>
    <HTTPTargetConnection>
        <URL>https://{private.splunkURL}/{private.splunkResourcePath}</URL>
    </HTTPTargetConnection>
</ServiceCallout>
Raise Fault

This policy raises a fault for error responses returned from the target system. It preserves the backend status code and response content so that consumers receive a meaningful error while the same transaction is also available in Splunk for support analysis. Care should be taken when using this policy to ensure that sensitive backend payload content is not exposed to clients or written to logs without review.

Condition: Use (message.status.code =| 4 or message.status.code =| 5).

This condition checks whether the response belongs to an error category, specifically HTTP 4xx or 5xx status codes. It ensures that the policy is triggered only for error responses, which makes it useful for exception handling and error-specific logging.

<!-- can be used to create custom messages in case of an error condition -->
<RaiseFault async="true" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" xmlns="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">
    <!-- Defines the response message returned to the requesting client -->
    <FaultResponse>
        <Set>
            <!-- Sets or overwrites HTTP headers in the respone message -->
            <Headers />
            <Payload contentType="application/xml">
                {response.content}
            </Payload>
            <StatusCode>{target.response.status.code}</StatusCode>
            <!-- sets the reason phrase of the response -->
            <!--<ReasonPhrase>Bad Request</ReasonPhrase>-->
        </Set>
    </FaultResponse>
    <IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>true</IgnoreUnresolvedVariables>
</RaiseFault>
Logging Behavior

The design supports two logging modes. If success logging is enabled for a proxy, both successful and failed API transactions are sent to Splunk. If success logging is disabled, only error scenarios such as authentication failures and 4xx/5xx responses are logged, which helps reduce logging volume while still preserving operational visibility for exceptions.

Conclusion

This solution enables SAP APIM transaction logging to Splunk for better monitoring of API errors, responses, and performance. By using KVM for secure configuration and logging key events in PreFlow and PostFlow, it gives the integration team flexible control over whether to capture only errors or all transactions in Splunk.

Limitations 

One limitation of this approach is that the success payload cannot be logged when streaming is enabled. This should be considered during design if payload-level success logging is a monitoring requirement.

entry.title: SuccessFactors PMGM Certification
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I am struggling with the PMGM certification exam. I work with certified PMGM consultants and know the material, but I keep getting exactly 25% on the system-based exam. I suspect I am missing something technical in the system configuration. I have also seen a few reddit posts of other users experiencing the same issue where they receive 25% each try.
  1. I’ve heard I need to use an assigned Group Number to replace '###' placeholders. Can anyone tell me exactly where to find this number during the exam? I have not found any in my test.
  2. I was told not to use a VPN,  but received the same score.
  3. I have opened cases but receive generic responses. How can I get a one-on-one session to understand why the automated grader is marking my work wrong?
Any help from those who have passed the system-based version recently is appreciated.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Security: How to realize client-credentials flow with IAS [4]: mTLS with X509_PROVIDED

Question..
I have read that SAP IAS supports direct trust - meaning our certificate doesn't need to be signed by a public CA like DigiCert. Direct trust means that whether the certificate is self-signed or CA signed, IAS is going to trust it without verifying the issuer against a global trust list.. 
Do you know if this is correct?
If yes, what needs to be done to make this work?  I couldn't make it work by simply creating a self-signed cert using OPENSSL. It's rejected. 

I'm in IAS Sandbox.. Does this not support this feature?

entry.title: Defining BELNR and XBLNR intervals for a manually created bank statement
entry.content:
Hi all,

How can we ensure BELNR and XBLNR intervals are defined for a bank statement that is created manually through Manage Bank Statements > Create?

We have a recurring issue with one of our bank statements in which transactions are being automatically allocated to the wrong debtor accounts. This particular bank statement is created manually by uploading an Excel file through Manage Bank Statement > Create. 

In the past we had this issue with our other bank accounts but resolved it by restricting the number ranges under Electronic Bank Statements > Parameter Set Definition. However, the issue persists with the bank statement we are creating manually, so it seems to be bypassing these settings.

This note 2979670 - Error : "The difference is too large for clearing" in Reprocess Bank Statement Items app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me advises how to restrict the number range intervals when using Upload Bank Statements and Manage Incoming Payment Files, but does not mention how to do it when using the Manage Bank Statement app.

We are using S4Hana Cloud Public.

Also - we're not interested in setting up processing rules at the moment - the posting rules and interpretation algorithms are working fine for the bank statements where the defined number ranges aren't being ignored.

Is anyone able to please advise?

entry.title: Re: Manage Service Orders & Maintenance Order within SSAM...
entry.content:
In a typical SAP S/4HANA utilities setup, you do not need separate mobile apps or FSM companies—both EAM (maintenance) and IS-U field processes (service) can be handled within a single SAP Service and Asset Manager application by configuring different work order types, roles, and workflows for emergencies, outage, maintenance like inspections, surveys, leak management since most IS-U activities are executed through order-based processes integrated with EAM; similarly, a single SAP Field Service Management company is sufficient, where you can manage different scenarios using planning groups, service types, and organizational structures, while SAP S/4HANA remains the system of record for both Asset Management and IS-U.

Hope this helps !

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now - Trainer Playback
entry.content:
Hi Melissa,

You must make sure that the toolbar is turned on for all Simulations modes where you want it to appear.  It's turned on for Demo mode by default but turned off for Practice and Test modes.  You probably would leave it turned off for Test mode, if you are using that mode, but do the following to turn it on for Practice mode:

1. Open Producer
2. Start editing the Playback Settings object and then double click it to open it
3. Click on the Practice Mode object to modify the settings for Practice Mode Simulations
4. If you are using a custom Trainer, open the Visual Properties area of settings
5. Click the checkbox in front of the Style setting, until it is green, and select your custom Trainer style

 

6. Open the Control Bar section of settings
7. Turn on the Hide Control Bar setting and make sure that it is deselected (no tick)
8. Turn on the Hide Navigation Button setting and make sure that it is deselected


 


9. Close the Playback Settings dialog box and synchronize the modified Playback Settings object to Manager
10 Publish the Playback Settings object

You should now see the toolbar in Practice mode.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Title: Dump MESSAGE_TYPE_X / CUIB1 007 when creating...
entry.content:
To narrow down why configuration works in CU50 but dumps in ME51N, could you please confirm a few details?

  1. In ME51N, is the Plant filled before you open the configuration, or do you enter it afterwards / does it get defaulted later?
  2. In CU41, what is the processing mode of the configuration profile (Classic LO-VC vs Advanced/AVC)?
  3. Are there any active enhancements/BAdIs for PR creation (e.g., ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST, MEREQ001, custom subscreens, exits) that might change fields like plant/item category during processing?
  4. If possible, could you share the call stack section from ST22 (top few frames) for the dump?

With these answers, it would be easy to pinpoint whether this is a context-change issue, enhancement interference, or a known SAP defect/note.

entry.title: Re: You cannot edit this employee time anymore because it...
entry.content:
Hi Bea_Bamboo, 

EmployeeTime's Editable field is set to No when Period End Processing BR is triggered (i.e. Time Account is set to Closed). 

Reopen the time account doesn't revert the Editable field value automatically.

This is a standard system behaviour to control retroactive change on the Leave Request (as it has consumed the said quota in the time account).

Import & Export Data is required for mass update. Please refer to the content in https://me.sap.com/notes/0002162407

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to split PPV when posting a Goods Rece...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, Purchase Price Variances (PPV) cannot be split across multiple G/L accounts the same way production order variances can. Instead, all PPVs are posted to the single price difference account (PRD), but SAP provides categorization through the Subledger-Specific Line Item Type (SLALITTYPE) field, which allows reporting and analysis by variance type.

Having said that, in Public Cloud, SAP deliberately restricts customizing options. PPVs are posted to the single PRD account. You can still analyze them by variance category (via the SLALITTYPE field in ACDOCA), but you cannot configure multiple G/L accounts for PPV splitting. This is a design choice to keep the system standardized and reduce complexity. Check this SAP Help Event-Based Variances

Challenge:

  • User-specific authorization for posting in different periods

entry.title: Re: FB03 - PAYMENT USAGE
entry.content:
Workarounds for your case

1) Use FAGLL03 Instead of FBL5N
Navigate to FAGLL03 and select the reconciliation account for customers. You can drill down into individual line items, similar to FBL5N, but with better performance.

2) FB03 Drilldown Remapping
FB03 “payment usage” tries to call the old line item display (FBL5N). In Public Cloud, you can configure navigation targets so that FB03 links to FAGLL03 or FBL5H instead. This avoids the MSITEM037 authorization error.

3) Authorizations
Ensure users have the correct business catalog for line item display apps. For example:

  • SAP_FIN_BC_GL_LINEITEM_PC (for FAGLL03)
  • SAP_FIN_BC_AR_AP_LINEITEM_PC (for FBL5H)

Without these, the drilldown will fail even if the app exists.

4) Fiori Alternatives
Use the Fiori app Display Line Items in General Ledger (App ID F2217), which is essentially FAGLL03 in Fiori. For customer-specific views, Display Customer Line Items (FBL5H) is the browser-optimized replacement.

entry.title: Working with Git commands
entry.content:
Introduction:

Git is a decentralized version control system, which assists developers to follow the evolution of their code and cooperate effectively with other developers. It runs on a series of basic instructions like git init, git add, git commit, and git push that enables the user to manipulate versions of his or her code. With such commands, developers are able to have a full history of changes, work on multiple branches, and merge updates without any problems.

Body:

To create repository and sync it with Visual Studio , Please refer my blog https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-blogs-posts/ai-steps-to-setup-python-programming/ba-p/14381631

Open a new terminal in VS code

Create Python1.py in VsCode

Get into your repository folder. 

cd {fodlername}

List all items with “”

git status

Add my folders into local repository

git add *

Commit the changes to Git staging area

Git commit -m {Tag}

Cross check with Github repository. Nothing added

 

 

Move from staging area to Github repository

git push

Content added to Github repository

 

Remove file

git rm {filename}

Changes staged 

git commit -m {Tag}

Move to Github repository

git push

 

Empty folder not shown in Git

Summary:

Git is the distributed version control program which helps trace the alterations in the code and allows effective co-operation of the developers. It has easy to use commands to govern versions, maintain history, and allow easy branching and merging.

entry.title: Re: Lessons Learned from Hypercare to AMS Transition in S...
entry.content:
Common Challenges

1) Peak load blind spots
KT sessions often focus on “steady state” scenarios, but AMS teams encounter issues during month-end close, fiscal year-end, or interface spikes that weren’t fully tested.

2) Incomplete documentation of workarounds
Temporary fixes or manual monitoring steps used during hypercare sometimes don’t make it into formal KT, leaving AMS teams guessing later.

3) Monitoring Gaps
Hypercare teams may rely on ad-hoc monitoring, while AMS needs structured tools and thresholds. Transitioning this knowledge is often overlooked.

4) Coordination Drop-off
Once the project team exits, AMS may lose access to functional experts who understand the business nuances behind certain errors.


Best Practices for smoother transition

1) Simulate Peak Cycles During Hypercare
Run mock month-end or interface load tests so AMS sees the system under stress before takeover.

2) Document Temporary Workarounds Explicitly
Even if they’re not “final solutions,” capture them in KT docs so AMS knows what was done and why.

3) Define Critical Monitoring Windows
Identify periods (e.g., 1st–5th of month, nightly batch runs) and set up dashboards or alerts for AMS to focus on.

4) Joint troubleshooting sessions
Let AMS shadow the project team during hypercare incidents so they build confidence in handling similar cases later.

5) Lessons learned workshops
At the end of hypercare, hold a structured session to capture what went wrong, what was fixed, and what still needs attention.

6) Gradual ownership transfer
Instead of a hard cutover, use a phased approach where AMS handles simpler tickets first, then progressively takes on complex issues with project team support.

So, the key takeaway from the above is operational readiness is not just about KT and sign-offs; it is about exposing AMS to real production behavior and ensuring every workaround, monitoring step, and escalation path is captured.

entry.title: Re: Assignment of Existing Sales Group to Another Sales O...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the assignment of a Sales Group to a Sales Office is a one-to-one relationship. Once a Sales Group is tied to a Sales Office in CBC (Configuration via Central Business Configuration), you cannot assign the same Sales Group to multiple Sales Offices. This is a deliberate restriction in the Public Cloud design to keep organizational structures standardized and avoid overlapping responsibilities.

Workarounds / Alternatives

1) Create a New Sales Group
If you need the same team to operate under multiple Sales Offices, you can replicate the Sales Group definition and assign it separately. This keeps reporting clean but requires duplicate master data.

2) Use Sales Employee Assignment
Instead of reusing the Sales Group, assign individual sales employees to multiple Sales Offices. This gives flexibility at the personnel level while keeping the group-office link intact.

3) Reporting Layer Solution
In analytics (e.g., CDS views, embedded analytics, or SAC), you can group multiple Sales Groups together to simulate “shared responsibility” across offices. This avoids structural changes but provides visibility.

4) Escalation to SAP Influence
If your business case strongly requires one Sales Group across multiple Sales Offices, this is a limitation you’d raise via SAP Customer Influence program or an Improvement Request.

entry.title: Guidance Needed: Migrating Mutual Fund Investments from FI-AA (Asset Accounting) to SAP TRM (Treasur
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am looking for expert advice regarding a module transition. Currently, our company manages Mutual Fund investments as Fixed Assets (FI-AA). We have now configured the Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) module and want to move these investments there for better management.

Our current process is simple: we only record the Purchase and Retirement of these funds in SAP.

My Proposed Approach

Since we do not have Fiori apps available in our landscape, I am planning the following:

Asset Retirement: Retire all existing Mutual Fund assets in FI-AA to clear the Asset sub-ledger.

Data Migration: Use LSMW to bring the data into the Treasury module.

I need clarification on the following points:

Migration Object: What exactly should be migrated into TRM? Should I migrate the original historical posting documents, or just the current "Open Position" (quantity and value) as of the cut-over date?

Standard Uploaders: Aside from LSMW, is there a standard SAP technical uploader (like a BAPI or a specific functional transaction) designed to initialize positions in the Transaction Manager?

Reconciliation: Since we only handle purchases and retirements, what is the best way to ensure the GL stays balanced during this shift? For example, if I retire the asset to a "Clearing Account," should I then use that same clearing account for the "Purchase" entry in TRM?

Implementation Steps: Could someone provide a brief sequence of steps for this "Fixed Asset to TRM" move? I want to ensure I don't miss any critical integration settings between the two sub-ledgers.

A Simple Example: If we own 1,000 units of a Mutual Fund currently sitting in Asset Accounting, would the correct flow be to retire those 1,000 units in FI-AA today and "buy" them into TRM at the same cost price tomorrow to maintain continuity?

I would greatly appreciate any guidance or documentation from anyone who has handled a similar "Asset-to-Treasury" migration.

entry.title: Re: Testing SEPA payments, CAMT.053 bank statements and b...
entry.content:
Hi Tobias,

 

You are describing a very typical situation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition projects, especially in SEPA/EBICS landscapes. Your understanding is correct: PSD2 XS2A sandboxes are not designed for classic ERP payment and bank statement flows such as pain.001 or CAMT processing.

 

In practice, most projects use a combination of approaches rather than relying on a single end-to-end test system.

 

1. Testing inbound processes (CAMT.053)
Yes, manual or sample CAMT.053 files are commonly used for testing.
- Customers typically use sample files provided by banks or construct files based on ISO 20022 format
- These are uploaded into SAP (e.g. via Manage Bank Statements) to validate parsing, posting rules, and reconciliation

 

This is considered standard practice and is usually sufficient to validate the inbound logic independently from the bank connection.

 

2. Testing outbound payment files (pain.001)
For outbound payments:
- The focus is first on validating the generated payment file within SAP (structure, format, content)
- This step is usually done without any real bank interaction

 

If SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity is in scope, it can support:
- Message monitoring
- Basic validation of message flow
- End-to-end traceability

 

3. Testing the actual bank connection
This is where the main limitation exists: in many countries (including Germany), banks—especially with EBICS—often do not provide a full ERP-compatible sandbox.

 

As a result, projects typically use:
- Limited bank test environments (if available), or
- Controlled “penny tests” in production

 

Small-value transactions coordinated with the bank are widely accepted as the final validation step.

 

4. Availability of test systems or providers
There is generally no standard SAP-provided “demo bank” for full SEPA/CAMT end-to-end testing.
Some external providers or banks may offer partial testing capabilities, but:
- Availability varies significantly
- Support for classic ERP file-based processes is often limited

 

This is why projects rarely rely on a single external test environment.

 

5. Typical approach used in projects
In most SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementations:
- Sample CAMT.053 files are used for inbound testing
- Payment files are validated internally within SAP
- SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity (if used) provides technical validation and monitoring
- A controlled production test (penny test) is used for final end-to-end verification

 

6. Final point on end-to-end testing
Yes, in many real-world scenarios, a small controlled production transaction is the only way to fully validate the complete payment flow from ERP → MBC → Bank → MBC → ERP.

 

While not ideal, this is a standard and planned step in many implementations.
 
References : 
 Onboarding Process : https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/3fa51d2862684bcfa3183bf499792673/b689fad3a7864b9eaa88cb4b7742b58a.html

Connecting Multiple ERPs with Message Exchange Between Systems:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/3fa51d2862684bcfa3183bf499792673/21f19f0f706d4df4ac98f1b69fa61592.html

SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity:
 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_MULTI_BANK_CONNECTIVITY/3fa51d2862684bcfa3183bf499792673/6146e281e340429498d7f138eda3dea4.html

 

Best regards,
Mahendra C
MBC Team
